# Artisan

> Turn UI feel into design specs

## About

| field | value |
|---|---|
| slug | `artisan` |
| version | `1.0.0` |
| schema_version | `3` |
| author | 0xHoneyJar |
| license | MIT |

Turns 'this feels off' into a design specification. Breaks down UI into color, animation, and spacing — then gives you the exact values. 14 skills covering design feedback analysis, animation patterns, component architecture, and accessibility audits.

## Personas

- `@ALEXANDER` → [`identity/ALEXANDER.md`](identity/ALEXANDER.md)

## Skills

- `analyzing-feedback` — Analyze feedback logs to detect design preference patterns. Auto-contributes HIGH confidence patterns upstream.
- `animating-motion` — Motion design and animation patterns for UI based on Emil Kowalski's principles
- `applying-behavior` — Touch, keyboard, and form interaction patterns for accessible UI
- `crafting-physics` — Design physics system for UI interactions - sync strategies, timing, confirmations
- `decomposing-feel` — Convert vague "feel" feedback into specific actionable fixes via decomposition questions
- `distilling-components` — Component design patterns - compound components, composition, props API
- `envisioning-direction` — Interactive design direction through visual reference capture and tension resolution. Use when starting a new project or refining design direction.
- `inscribing-taste` — Apply brand taste tokens to components from taste.md
- `iterating-visuals` — Auto-screenshot design iteration loop with active feedback request. Use when making visual/style changes to components.
- `next-best-practices` — React and Next.js performance optimization patterns from Vercel Engineering
- `rams` — Accessibility and visual design review based on WCAG 2.1
- `styling-material` — UI polish patterns for typography, visual design, layout, and dark mode
- `surveying-patterns` — UI baseline constraints and pattern enforcement to prevent interface slop
- `synthesizing-taste` — Extract brand taste tokens from reference materials into taste.md
